places.name: For Sale Pub
places.description: This Budapest bar allows drinkers to leave their mark on the walls, floor, and even ceiling.

places.name: Panoptikum Budapest
places.description: A series of caves under Castle Hill that once held the prisoner Vlad Tepes, better known as Count Dracula.

places.name: Szabo Ervin Library
places.description: A 19th-century artistocrat's mansion, turned into a library, hidden in a modern library.

places.name: Shoes on the Danube Promenade
places.description: A trail of iron footwear stands as a monument to the thousands executed along this riverbank during WWII.

places.name: Budapest Castle Hill Funicular
places.description: Built in 1870 at the bridge that connected Buda and Pest, this spectacular incline railway was almost lost forever during World War II.

places.name: Vajdahunyad Castle
places.description: This Hungarian castle was built out of cardboard and wood until it proved so popular that it got upgraded to stone.

places.name: Budapest Cave Church
places.description: Once the home of a hermetic monk, this cave now houses a small church.

places.name: Szimpla Kert
places.description: A Trabant car, a kangaroo statue, and a plethora of plants take over this abandoned-factory-turned-bar.

places.name: Central Café and Restaurant 1887
places.description: Some of the greatest minds in the history of Hungarian art and science converged in this café.

places.name: Zwack Unicum Museum
places.description: Central Europe's largest collection of mini-bottles is on display at this museum devoted to a bitter Hungarian liquor.
